mechanism_narrative: Sustained year-over-year growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If over any 24-month rolling window, the 12-month growth rate of RSAFS has a negative correlation with forward 20-day returns (rank IC < 0.0), this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% from current 4.1% signals labor market deterioration that will reduce aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 4% and retail sales decline month-over-month, the combination of tight monetary policy and weakening consumer demand amplifies recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If instances satisfying both conditions do not produce significantly negative average 20-day forward returns (t-test p < 0.10) compared to unconditional mean, or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS above its current 4.33%), a decline in industrial production indicates a broader economic slowdown that will reduce corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If during monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.33%), the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60-day returns is not significantly positive (i.e., INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns with rank IC < -0.05 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 10% signals a housing market downturn that directly reduces construction employment and consumer confidence, leading to negative weekly equity returns as markets price in lower growth.
failure_condition: If after a >10% monthly decline in HOUST, the average 5-day forward return is not negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10) or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55% over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment is rising and the unemployment rate is concurrently below 4%, the labor market is extremely tight, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If instances with rising PAYEMS and UNRATE < 4% do not yield significantly positive average 20-day forward returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or if the joint condition's effect is not greater than the sum of individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds the 320 level, inflation is perceived as high, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, the 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05) over any 5-year window with at least 20 occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production over three consecutive months signals ongoing economic expansion, leading to increased corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If after a streak of three consecutive monthly gains in INDPRO, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the win rate is below 55% over historical data.
